patterns_recognized: D-grade dual-signal failure pattern (SP-edge-against + PQI-against) remains 7-for-9 directionally wrong through 5/21 — CLE@PHI, TB@NYY, SEA@KC, and WSH@ATL all qualify today; treating all four as expected wrong direction.

TB@NYY: NYY top-4 batters (Judge, Rice, Bellinger, Goldschmidt) all WEAK_VS vs Rasmussen's opponent Weathers (10 PA each, shrunk ~0.665), compounding the D-grade structural case for the third consecutive same-series series loss at NYY for TOR/TB.

Extreme Stage 1/2 delta (CHW@SF: f5_full_delta=0.4486) combined with model_prob outside valid [0.48, 0.72] range signals calibration artifact — DOWNGRADE is warranted when the model's own probability is flagged as out-of-range.

HARD CAP 10 demoted two pre-cap A-grade picks (LAD@MIL score=5, TEX@LAA score=5) solely due to pick_prob in (0.50, 0.55]; CONFIRM rescues both — the elite SP quality (Yamamoto, Detmers) and multi-signal conviction argue for preserving the directional thesis at lower confidence.

BvP extreme patterns (SEA@KC: all 5 SEA batters OWNER vs Lugo, all 5 KC batters WEAK_VS vs Woo) provide qualitative reinforcement of D-grade directional failure labels — not a solo override trigger but strongly corroborative.

PIT @ TOR.reasoning: Dylan Cease (TOR home) carries a 2.03 xERA advantage over Mitch Keller (PIT away) — largest SP edge on the slate. PQI Δ=+18.0 confirms TOR's full-game profile. Stage 1/2 delta=0.234 is a significant concern: TOR wins F5 at 73.6% but full-game is coin-flip (0.502), reflecting bullpen-carry risk with hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0144 (TOR slightly weaker bullpen). HARD CAP 7 and CAP 10 both fired; CONFIRM rescues to B- (score=1). BvP: Lowe (OWNER vs Cease, shrunk 0.945) is an isolated PIT threat; Reynolds is WEAK_VS. Low-conviction CONFIRM on SP quality and PQI alignment.

Framber Valdez is an established quality starter who will challenge BAL's lineup (Henderson, Alonso, Rutschman). Grade C/SKIP is correct: without market validation there is no basis to elevate conviction. BvP: mostly NO_DATA for BAL vs Valdez. CONFIRM SKIP — market validation required before any directional commitment.

DET @ BAL (TBD).reasoning: Troy Melton has 0 Statcast pitches season-to-date (below 100-pitch minimum). PENDING_SP_DATA hard stop — SP quality is unquantifiable, making any directional call unreliable regardless of lineup or other signals. BAL lineup quality (Henderson, Alonso, Rutschman) is irrelevant without the SP anchor. No pick.

MIN @ BOS.reasoning: Bailey Ober (MIN away) has F1*=1.10 xERA over Sonny Gray (BOS home), though the asterisk flags thin Statcast sample. HARD CAP 5 correctly fired (F1* alone, no F2/F3 corroboration). Stage 1/2 agree (Δ=0.079) and pick_prob=0.510 make this marginally above .500. BvP: MIN top-5 show identical OWNER readings across all batters (all 20 PA, OPS=1.489) — this data pattern looks anomalous and should not be weighted. CONFIRM preserves score=2 (B) to prevent HARD CAP 10 zeroing. Lowest-conviction CONFIRM on the slate; F1* thin-sample is the primary risk.

CLE @ PHI.reasoning: SP edge 1.42 xERA AGAINST PHI (Parker Messick superior to Andrew Painter) + PQI Δ=−21.4 against PHI late-game profile = dual-signal D-grade failure pattern. Per 7-of-9 postgame directional record, PHI is expected wrong direction. PHI's lineup (Turner, Schwarber, Harper) is elite but BvP vs Messick is thin (mostly NO_DATA/SMALL_SAMPLE for PHI top-3). Dual primary signals both oppose the model's pick direction. CONFIRM SKIP — PHI direction is structurally flawed; treat as expected CLE win.

Stage 1/2 delta=0.136 flags bullpen-carry risk. hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0145 (NYY bullpen slightly worse). CONFIRM SKIP — structural case for NYY direction is weaker than any other D-grade SKIP today; TB direction strongly supported by BvP and serial-game pattern.

Stage 1/2 disagree (Δ=0.4486) combined with out-of-range model probability and fair_prob missing removes any market anchor. HARD CAP 7 fires on hl_bullpen_xwoba_gap=+0.0030 (near-zero, borderline application). Edge=+11.54pp in [4,15]pp band; fair_prob=0.5318 fetched — market validates LAA at 53.2%. Bullpen_fatigue flag on TEX is a secondary negative for TEX. Ezequiel Duran (TEX) is WEAK_VS vs Detmers (shrunk 0.525, 15 PA). This is the best market-validated pick on today's slate with all pre-cap grade conditions met and a market-anchored positive edge.
